characters.name: Agent Hill
characters.description: Agent HillAgent Hillis a character inSeason 2ofMelrose Place, portrayed by Sam Anderson. Agent Hill was a special agent with the Federal Bureau of Investigation. He was the lead investigator into the criminal activities ofPalmer Woodward. AfterJake Hansonwas approached by Hill and his partner, Hill coerced Jake into working with them and later wearing a wire to expose Palmer's criminal activities. After Palmer was arrested, Agent Hill did not press charges against Jake.

characters.name: Alex Bastian
characters.description: Alex BastianRelationshipsRomancesJane AndrewsBackstageEpisodes8Alex Bastianis a recurring character onMelrose Place. He is portrayed by Steve Wilder. Alex was a famous clothing designer and college classmate ofJane AndrewsandMichael Mancini. He had a one night stand with Jane the night before her first marriage to Michael. Ten years later, and when Jane was working forAmanda Woodward Advertising, he was a new client and the old flame put her reborn relationship with Michael at risk. characters.name: Alycia Barnett
characters.description: Alycia BarnettAlycia Barnettis a guest character onMelrose Place. She is portrayed by Anne-Marie Johnson. Alycia was a lawyer whose first appearance came when she was hired byThe Fieldingsto defendMatt Fieldingagainst murder charges when he was framed for the murder ofCarol Graham. She later removed herself from the case when Matt became reckless in trying to prove his innocence.  However, she was onhand when Paul confessed to the murder with his dying words. Alycia was a lawyer whose first appearance came when she was hired byThe Fieldingsto defendMatt Fieldingagainst murder charges when he was framed for the murder ofCarol Graham. She later removed herself from the case when Matt became reckless in trying to prove his innocence.  However, she was onhand when Paul confessed to the murder with his dying words. When Alycia returned later in the year, she was no longer a criminal attorney and representedBobby Pareziafter he dismissedNick Diamond.  She informedJake Hansonthat his debt with a loan shark had been paid by an anonymous benefactor, which was Bobby. She also had a brief romantic relationship withPeter Burns. When Bobby faced a slew of professional troubles, he became increasingly paranoid and violent, and eventually attempted to attack Alycia. This led to Bobby falling to his death when Alycia dodged his advance. Soon afterward, Alycia herself died in a car accident.

characters.name: Amanda Woodward
characters.description: Amanda WoodwardBirthday8 November 1963FromOakvilleOccupationPresident atWPKFORMERLYArt director atD&DVice president ofD&DPresident ofD&DPresident ofAWAOwner ofMelrose PlaceRelationshipsFamilyHillary Michaels(mother)David Michaels(half-brother)Palmer Woodward(father)RomancesJack Parezi(ex-husband)Peter Burns(husband)Kyle McBride(ex-husband)Rory Blake(ex-husband)Ken Damar,Bobby Parezi,Billy Campbell,Jake Hanson,Michael ManciniBackstagePortrayed byHeather LocklearSeriesMelrose PlaceFirst seenLast seenPicture ImperfectAsses to AshesEpisodes199 + 3 (Melrose Place (2009))Amanda Woodwardis a principal character onMelrose Place, portrayed byHeather Locklear. The daughter ofPalmer WoodwardandHillary Michaels, Amanda also had a younger half-brother,David Michaels. Following her experiences with parental neglect and domestic abuse, Amanda developed a hard shell and a sometimes testy nature. However, she retains a pleasant and compassionate side, which figures prominently into her character development. Amanda Woodward was introduced as an advertising executive atD&D, who eventually became the direct superior ofAlison Parker. Though the women at first got along, their relationship became strained after Amanda started dating Alison's roommateBilly—a development which brought Alison a sense of awkwardness. The ill feelings eventually began to reveal a more malicious side to Amanda's personality, which crossed over into the workplace. Billy was gradually drawn away from Amanda, and later began seeing Alison. Though Amanda was soon back on good terms with the couple, she then stunned them with her announcement that she was buying their apartment complex and moving in. The daughter ofPalmer WoodwardandHillary Michaels, Amanda also had a younger half-brother,David Michaels. Following her experiences with parental neglect and domestic abuse, Amanda developed a hard shell and a sometimes testy nature. However, she retains a pleasant and compassionate side, which figures prominently into her character development.

characters.name: Arthur Field
characters.description: Arthur FieldStatusDeceasedCause of deathHeart attackRelationshipsFamilyCraig Field(son)BackstagePortrayed byMichael Des BarresSeriesMelrose PlaceArthur Fieldis a character onMelrose Place. He is portrayed Michael Des Barres. Arthur was theD&D Advertisingchairman of the board and father ofCraig Field. He was very into office politics and favoredBilly Campbellafter he had become a no-nonsense, corrupt ad man. He was seen to have loved gossiping about the sex life ofAmanda Woodward, information which he passed on to his son when he came to work there. After Craig started and had conflict with Billy, Arthur demanded that Amanda fire Billy. Billy later overheard Arthur telling Craig to learn everything he could from Amanda before he destroyed her. Arthur was theD&D Advertisingchairman of the board and father ofCraig Field. He was very into office politics and favoredBilly Campbellafter he had become a no-nonsense, corrupt ad man. He was seen to have loved gossiping about the sex life ofAmanda Woodward, information which he passed on to his son when he came to work there. After Craig started and had conflict with Billy, Arthur demanded that Amanda fire Billy. Billy later overheard Arthur telling Craig to learn everything he could from Amanda before he destroyed her. However, after Craig learned he had controlling interest inD&D Advertising, Craig rebelled against his father and blackmailed him with the information that Arthur had accidentally killed his own father and had covered it up with Craig's help. He later died of a heart attack after arguing with Craig, in his attempt to buy Amanda off.

characters.name: Audrey Williams
characters.description: Audrey WilliamsOccupationNurse atWilshire Memorial HospitalRelationshipsRomancesMichael Mancini(affair)BackstagePortrayed byStephanie CameronSeriesMelrose PlaceSeason(s)Season 7Audrey Williamsis a recurring character onSeason 7ofMelrose Place. She is portrayed by Stephanie Cameron. Audrey was a nurse atWilshire Memorial Hospitalwho suedMichael Mancinifor sexual harassment after he failed to give her a promotion. However, it was later revealed to be a setup, with Michael and Audrey in a scam together to file a lawsuit against the hospital. Audrey went toPeter Burnsand told him she made everything up, giving Michael his reason to sue the hospital. She became a little more demanding as they waited for the settlement, wanting three-hundred thousand dollars, in addition to wanting to have an actual affair with Michael. When Michael tried to break up with her, she mailed a tape of she and Michael having sex to Peter, whichJane Mancinisaw and ended her marriage to Michael.

characters.description: Barry DenoffOccupationBoard Member atWilshire Memorial HospitalBackstagePortrayed byJoseph CampanellaSeriesMelrose PlaceSeason(s)Season 7Barry Denoffis a recurring character inSeason 7ofMelrose Place, portrayed by Joseph Campanella. Barry is a member of the board atWilshire Memorial Hospital. When Michael was accused of sexual harassment byAudrey Williams, Barry refused to back Michael and asked for his resignation without even hearing his side of the story. characters.name: Beth Davis
characters.description: Beth DavisCause of deathEuthanasia byPeter BurnsRelationshipsFamilyTaylor McBride(sister)RomancesPeter Burns(widower)BackstagePortrayed byLisa Ann HadleyBeth Davisis a character inSeason 5ofMelrose Place. She is portrayed by Lisa Ann Hadley. Beth is the deceased wife ofPeter Burnsand the sister ofTaylor McBride. Beth only appeared in flashbacks and dream sequences, but her existence served as a catalyst for the demise of Peter's marriage toAmanda Woodward. While Beth was married to Peter, she was diagnosed with cancer.  Peter stated she was slowly slipping away and begged for him to release her from her pain.  Eventually, he honored her request and euthanized her.  However, Taylor fixed it to make it look like suicide so Peter wouldn't got to jail. characters.name: Billy Campbell
characters.description: Billy CampbellBornWilliam Campbell Jr.Birthday1969FromCaliforniaOccupationWriter • Publicist atD&DStatusAliveRelationshipsFamilyWilliam Campbell, Sr.(father)Joan Campbell(mother)Celeste Campbell(sister)RomancesAlison Parker(ex-fiancée/soulmate)Brooke Armstrong† (ex-wife)Samantha Reilly(ex-wife)Amanda WoodwardSusan MadsenJennifer Mancini(girlfriend)Elizabeth WyattMarcy GarrettDawn BondsBackstagePortrayed byAndrew ShueSeriesMelrose PlaceFirst seenLast seenPilotBuona Sera, Mr. Campbell: Part 2Episodes198Billy Campbellis a principal character onMelrose Place, portrayed byAndrew Shue. In the first episode, Billy approachedAlison Parkerin response to her search for a new roommate at Melrose Place. Alison was initially reluctant, and the two got off to a comically negative start. When Alison was unable to find a more compatible match, however, she tracked Billy down and politely took him up on the offer. Billy later came to Alison's aid one night when he found a man from her job making unwelcome advances in their living room. He peacefully drove the man away while posing as Alison's husband, which brought the two new roommates closer. Alison grew uncomfortable when Billy started datingAmanda Woodward, an executive at her job who she worked closely with. In time, the resentment between the two women drove Billy and Amanda apart. Though initially an aspiring novelist, Billy went to work for a magazine as a staff writer. He was the object of sexual harassment by his boss,Nancy Donnerand had a love-hate relationship with co-workerCelia Morales. Later, he found his calling in advertising, and began working alongside Alison and Amanda at theD&Dagency. Billy was comforted by several of his friends, including Amanda, following the death of his father. In time, he and Alison began dating. Shortly afterward, though, Amanda revealed that she was pregnant with Billy's child. However, she later miscarried.

characters.name: Bob Wilkens
characters.description: Bob WilkensBackstagePortrayed byMark MetcalfSeriesMelrose PlaceSeason(s)Season 3Bob Wilkensis a recurring character seen inSeason 3ofMelrose Place, portrayed by Mark Metcalf. Bob was an internal affairs detective within the Los Angeles Police Department. He came to interviewJo Reynoldsfollowing an incident with DetectiveJohn Rawlingsin which he beat a suspect into a coma. Matt Fieldinglater brought Wilkens photos that Jo took of the incident, which implicated Rawlings in the wrongful death of a suspect. characters.name: Bobby Parezi
characters.description: Bobby PareziOccupationMarineStatusDeceasedRelationshipsFamilyJack Parezi(brother)RomancesSydney AndrewsBackstagePortrayed byJohn Enos IIISeriesMelrose PlaceBobby Pareziis a recurring character on FOX drama soapMelrose Place. He is portrayed by John Enos III. Bobby is the brother ofJack Parezi, who wasAmanda Woodward's first husband. He is the only known member of the crime-ridden Parezi family who strived to make an honest living. Originally in the Navy, he fought in the Gulf War, considered the good son in comparison to Jack. Following Jack's death during a struggle with Amanda, Bobby was sent byhis fatherto take vengeance. Instead, Bobby developed a brief romantic relationship with Amanda and found himself unable to harm her. Bobby is the brother ofJack Parezi, who wasAmanda Woodward's first husband. characters.name: Brandi Carson
characters.description: Brandi CarsonRelationshipsFamilyTeri CarsonRomancesMichael ManciniBackstagePortrayed byDenise RichardsSeriesMelrose PlaceSeason(s)Season 4Episodes3Brandi Carsonis a recurring character inSeason 4ofMelrose Place, portrayed by Denise Richards. Brandi was a contestant in the "Miss Health and Fitness" beauty pageant being judged by Michael Mancini and Peter Burns. She and her mother,Teri Carson, schemed to have Brandi declared the winner by seducing the judges. Brandi successfully seduced Michael into a one-night stand. However, although Michael voted for Brandi, Peter did not and she ended up coming in second place. Her mother then decided to sue Peter for having sex with a minor, despite it being Michael that did it. Brandi tried to call Michael and expressed guilt over the lawsuit, but was being forced by her mother to continue with it. After the lawsuit was dropped after Amanda Woodward gave Brandi a job on a big ad campaign, Brandi informed Amanda that everything was a lie.

characters.name: Brett Cooper
characters.description: Brett CooperFromCleveland, OHOccupationSurgeonStatusAliveRelationshipsFamilyMs. Cooper (sister)RomancesLexi SterlingKimberly ShawMegan LewisBackstagePortrayed byLinden AshbySeriesMelrose PlaceSeason(s)Season 6First seenLast seenA Brand New DayBuona Sera, Mr. Campbell: Part 2Episodes34Brett Cooperis a principal character on FOX drama soapMelrose Place. He is portrayed byLinden Ashby. He was called Coop, was a surgeon, and was married toLexi Sterlingtwice. While first married to Lexi, Brett had an affair with Dr.Kimberly Shaw, who he'd fallen for while helping her recover from a car crash. After his marriage to Lexi ended, Brett moved to Los Angeles and eventually began seeingMegan Lewis. However, he soon learned that Lexi's rich father had left him an inheritance, which he could only receive by remarrying her. Brett initially declined, but later discovered that Lexi had been set up to take the fall in her father's embezzlement scheme, and needed five million dollars as a result. To help solve this problem, he entered a platonic marriage with Lexi, and both claimed the inheritance. He was called Coop, was a surgeon, and was married toLexi Sterlingtwice. While first married to Lexi, Brett had an affair with Dr.Kimberly Shaw, who he'd fallen for while helping her recover from a car crash. After his marriage to Lexi ended, Brett moved to Los Angeles and eventually began seeingMegan Lewis. However, he soon learned that Lexi's rich father had left him an inheritance, which he could only receive by remarrying her. Brett initially declined, but later discovered that Lexi had been set up to take the fall in her father's embezzlement scheme, and needed five million dollars as a result. To help solve this problem, he entered a platonic marriage with Lexi, and both claimed the inheritance. In time, Lexi managed to seduce Brett, which prompted Megan to leave him. Highly resentful, Brett lured Lexi on a sailing trip and attempted to kill her during a storm. However, Megan—who'd secretly followed along after growing suspicious—appeared and managed to save Lexi's life. Brett then departed town, leaving the two women to become friends.

characters.name: Brittany Maddocks
characters.description: Brittany MaddocksRelationshipsRomancesJake HansonBackstagePortrayed byKathy IrelandSeriesMelrose PlaceSeason(s)Season 3First seenI Am Curious MelroseBrittany Maddocksis a character inSeason 3ofMelrose Place, portrayed by Kathy Ireland. Brittany was a woman hired byPalmer Woodwardto set upJake Hansonin an attempt to murder him. Brittany seduced Jake with a story about an abusive husband in order to set explosives onto his boat. However, Palmer came aboard and she turned on him, shooting him before offering Jake the chance to run away with her. Jake refused and she blew up the boat, but not before Jake was able to get away. She was later captured by the F.B.I.

Brooke, a member of the wealthy Armstrong family, is the daughter ofHayleyand Laurel Armstrong. In 1995, she becomes a new employee atD&Dadvertising agency. Though she initially showed a pleasant side, Brooke would gradually reveal a cocky, snobbish, malevolent and mischievous nature. A love triangle eventually developed between Brooke,Billy, andAlison, with Brooke manipulating Billy into thinking that Alison was no longer interested in him. She also used her father's power in the company to have Alison relocated to Hong Kong. Brooke, a member of the wealthy Armstrong family, is the daughter ofHayleyand Laurel Armstrong. characters.name: Bruce Teller
characters.description: Bruce TellerOccupationCEO atD&DBackstagePortrayed byStanley KamelSeriesMelrose PlaceSeason(s)2•3Bruce Telleris a recurring character onMelrose Place, portrayed byStanley Kamel. Generally good-natured, Bruce was the chief executive at theD&DAdvertising Agency. He worked closely withAmanda Woodward, as well asAlison ParkerandBilly Campbell. Eventually, Bruce entered a downward personal spiral, and his relationship with Amanda became strained—which was furthered when a group of doctors, led byPeter Burns, obtained a controlling interest in D&D. Amanda, who was romantically involved with Peter at the time, was quickly given Bruce's position in the company. When Bruce expressed anger, Amanda responded with a glib remark. Later, Amanda was shocked to find that Bruce had committed suicide in her office, leaving a note congratulating her on the promotion. Following Bruce's passing, Amanda's sensitive nature began to arise again.

Caitlin was hired as an efficiency expert atD&D AdvertisingbyPeter Burnsduring his brief involvement with the company. Being Peter's girlfriend, her goal was to takeAmanda Woodward's place as president of the agency. She was fired when Peter briefly went to jail for trying to kill Amanda. characters.name: Calvin Hobbs
characters.description: Calvin HobbsOccupationChief of staff atWilshire Memorial HospitalBackstagePortrayed byFrancis X. McCarthySeason(s)3,4Calvin Hobbsis a recurring character onMelrose Place. He is portrayed by Francis X. McCarthy. Dr. Hobbs was the chief-of-staff atWilshire Memorial Hospital, taking over afterPeter Burnswas fired. His first order of business was ordering a psychological evaluation on everyone at the hospital, which did not sit well withKimberly Shaw. Later on, he would be disgusted by her divorce withMichael Mancini. AfterMatt Fieldingwas cleared off all charges on the murder ofCarol Graham, Hobbs fired Matt on the day he returned to work because he felt he had ruined the reputation of the hospital. He was later sued by Matt andTom Rileyfor discrimination and during depositions, started blurting out bigoted statements against gay people, thereby losing his case and his position in the hospital.

characters.name: Carl Canin
characters.description: Carl CaninBackstageSeriesMelrose PlaceOnly seenCollision CourseCarl Caninis a character inSeason 2ofMelrose Place, portrayed by Stephen Nichols. Carl was a successful movie director who tried to purchase the services ofSydney Andrewsbecause he believed her to be a prostitute under the employ of madamLauren Ethridge. At first, he was kind and friendly, but after taking Sydney back to his home for sex, he turned abrasive and demanding. Sydney eventually ran away from him and left him calling her names.

characters.name: Carrie Fellows
characters.description: Carrie FellowsOccupationOrganisational ProfessionalStatusAliveBackstagePortrayed byRae Dawn ChongSeriesMelrose PlaceSeason(s)Season 1Carrie Fellowsis a guest character onSeason 1ofMelrose Place. She is portrayed by Rae Dawn Chong. Carrie becameRhonda Blair's first roommate afterSandy Harlingmoved to New York. Carrie didn't smoke. She didn't drink. She didn't have pets or a boyfriend. She loved to cook and she had a steady job. Carrie was an O.P. — an organisational professional. She helped arrange people's lives for them. She owned her own car. Her parents lived out of town. She believed toothpaste belonged on a brush and not in the sink. Carrie becameRhonda Blair's first roommate afterSandy Harlingmoved to New York. Carrie didn't smoke. She didn't drink. She didn't have pets or a boyfriend. She loved to cook and she had a steady job. Carrie was an O.P. — an organisational professional. She helped arrange people's lives for them. She owned her own car. Her parents lived out of town. She believed toothpaste belonged on a brush and not in the sink. Carrie ended up being O.C.D., rearranging everything in the house, which got on Rhonda's nerves. After it became too much, Rhonda asked Carrie to move out, which she did gladly. Carrie returned later in the season when Rhonda was planning her wedding toTerrence Haggard. Terrence was more welcoming of Carrie than Rhonda, so much so that they hired her to plan their wedding. Rhonda was convinced Carrie was coming after Terrence, but Terrence wasn't interested in Carrie.

After it became too much, Rhonda asked Carrie to move out, which she did gladly. Carrie returned later in the season when Rhonda was planning her wedding toTerrence Haggard. Terrence was more welcoming of Carrie than Rhonda, so much so that they hired her to plan their wedding. Rhonda was convinced Carrie was coming after Terrence, but Terrence wasn't interested in Carrie.

characters.name: Carter Gallavan
characters.description: Carter GallavanOccupationSoftware developerStatusAliveRelationshipsRomancesSydney AndrewsBackstagePortrayed byChad LoweSeriesMelrose PlaceCarter Gallavanis a recurring character onMelrose Place. He is portrayed by Chad Lowe. Carter was a billionaire software developer who fell in love withSydney Andrews. He was supposed to be the next Bill Gates. He and Sydney met when she overbid his bodyguard,Walter, on one ofSamantha Reilly's paintings by mistake. He bought it from her and gave her a thousand dollar profit. He called Sydney later and invited her to a benefit, which she accepted. Carter was sheltered and had grown disenchanted with his way of living. He found Sydney to be a breath of fresh air and a distraction from his normal everyday routine. Carter was a billionaire software developer who fell in love withSydney Andrews. He was supposed to be the next Bill Gates. He and Sydney met when she overbid his bodyguard,Walter, on one ofSamantha Reilly's paintings by mistake. He bought it from her and gave her a thousand dollar profit. He called Sydney later and invited her to a benefit, which she accepted. Carter was sheltered and had grown disenchanted with his way of living. He found Sydney to be a breath of fresh air and a distraction from his normal everyday routine. After several attempts to spend time with Sydney, business (andWalter) kept interfering, so Carter took Sydney to his childhood home in San Francisco to spend time alone without telling Walter where he was going. Carter and Sydney slept together for the first time on the trip, where he opened up about his last girlfriend wanting him to change and be more of a grownup, but her demands caused him to hurt his video game business. However, after he and Sydney had sex, he became inspired and couldn't do anything but work, causing Sydney to have go back home. Later, Walter made Sydney go away and poisoned Carter's mind against her by making up lies about her. Sydney later showed up at Carter's press conference for his new game and exposed Walter as a fraud, albeit through a veiled series of questions. Carter understood, however, and got the message. He later ran away from his life to come stay with Sydney. After Walter came looking for him, he fired him and took a job atKyle's, but after getting mugged in the parking lot, but saved byKyle McBride, Carter decided to go back into his safe world. He later overheard a conversation between Sydney andSamantha Reillyin which she confessed she didn't love Carter the way she should. Carter then politely broke up with her and flew away on his jet.

characters.name: Celeste Campbell
characters.description: Celeste CampbellStatusAliveRelationshipsFamilyBilly Campbell(brother)Joan Campbell(mother)William Campbell(father)BackstagePortrayed byRenée ColemanSeriesMelrose PlaceSeason(s)Season 1Only seenEnd GameCeleste Campbellis a guest character inSeason 1ofMelrose Place, portrayed by Renée Coleman. Celeste is the sister ofBilly Campbell. After the death of their father,William Campbell, when Billy was uncertain of whether his father actually loved him, Celeste was the one who convinced Billy that he did love Billy and was very proud of him. She and Joan later did what was best for Billy by selling the family business when Billy was going to abandon all of his dreams to run it for his father.

characters.name: Celia Morales
characters.description: Celia MoralesOccupationStaff writerStatusAliveBackstagePortrayed byMelanie SmithSeriesMelrose PlaceSeason(s)Season 2Episodes5Celia Moralesis a character inSeason 2ofMelrose Place, portrayed by Melanie Smith. Celia was a co-worker ofBilly Campbell, who was a staff writer at the magazine where they worked. They frequently exchanged banter, as Celia was quite annoyed Billy at the onset of their meeting. They frequently had to co-write articles at the request ofNancy Donner, at one point having to temporarily move into the apartment of Billy andAlison Parker. Celia was a co-worker ofBilly Campbell, who was a staff writer at the magazine where they worked. They frequently exchanged banter, as Celia was quite annoyed Billy at the onset of their meeting. They frequently had to co-write articles at the request ofNancy Donner, at one point having to temporarily move into the apartment of Billy andAlison Parker. Weeks later, Billy and Celia were both up for the same promotion as a junior editor and Billy won out over Celia.  Billy then moved to New York, quit the job, and returned to try to reclaim his old job.  Celia was upset with him because they wouldn't give her the job because he gave the L.A. office a bad name

characters.name: Charles Reynolds
characters.description: Charles ReynoldsStatusAliveRelationshipsRomancesJo Reynolds(ex-wife)BackstagePortrayed byLinden AshbySeason(s)Season 1Charles Reynoldsis a guest character inSeason 1ofMelrose Place. He is portrayed by Linden Ashby. Charles is the brash and successful ex-husband ofJo Reynoldsfrom New York. He came from a very wealthy family and was married to Jo for four years before she left without even a note. Their marriage ended due to his tendency to become drunken and abusive. Not long after Jo had moved intoMelrose Place, Charles came to town seeking a friendly reunion, and appeared to be a changed man. Eventually, however, his drunken ways emerged once again, partly motivated by the knowledge that Jo was seeingsomeone. During a brief scuffle, Jo had to fend Charles off one night in her apartment, prompting him to leave in an arrogant, intoxicated rage. characters.name: Chas Russell
characters.description: Chas RussellStatusAliveRelationshipsRomancesHillary MichaelsBackstagePortrayed byJeff KaakeSeriesMelrose PlaceSeason(s)Season 2Chas Russellis a guest character inSeason 2ofMelrose Place. He is portrayed by Jeff Kaake. Chas is the boyfriend ofHillary Michaelswho tried to cause trouble between Hillary and her estranged daughter,Amanda Woodward. While Amanda and Hillary were reconciling, Hillary recommended Chas for a job atD&D Advertisingand Amanda hired him as a junior executive. He immediately started slacking off and pushing all of his work off onAlison Parker. Later, he kissed Amanda, which Alison witnessed. She and a drunkenBilly Campbelllater let it slip toJake Hansonabout the kiss and he confronted Amanda. Consequently, Amanda fired Chas. characters.name: Chelsea Fielding
characters.description: Chelsea FieldingRelationshipsFamilyMatt FieldingBackstagePortrayed byKatie WrightSeriesMelrose PlaceSeason(s)5•6Chelsea Fieldingis a recurring character onMelrose Place, portrayed by Katie Wright. Chelsea was the 16-year old niece ofMatt Fielding, who became her guardian once her father, Luke, died in a plane accident. Her mother,Denise Fielding, had abandoned her when she was younger, leaving Chelsea no place to live. Luke willed Chelsea into Matt's custody, butConstance Fieldingfelt like Chelsea shoul come live with her and Matt agreed. However, Chelsea did not want to live with her and Matt agreed to let her stay with him for a short time. However, she proved to be full of attitude and a handful for Matt when she almost set the apartment on fire by leaving a cigarette on the couch and arguing withAmanda Woodward.
